name: Chocolate Fruitcake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Calimyrna figs; stemmed and diced, dried-about 7-1/2 oz
1/2 cup dark rum or spiced rum
1 cup mincemeat with brandy or rum
2 tablespoons dark rum or spiced rum
recipeInstructions:
For Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees and position rack in center.
2. Generously butter a 12-cup bundt pan, then dust with cocoa powder and tap out excess.
3. Combine the first six ingredients; let stand for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Sift flour, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt into a medium bowl.
5. Finely grind sugar and chopped chocolate in a processor.
6. Beat butter until fluffy using an electric mixer. Add the sugar mixture in three additions, beating to blend after each addition.
7. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in 1/3 of the dry ingredients with a rubber spatula.
8. Mix in half of the mincemeat. Add remaining dry ingredients and walnuts; toss to coat evenly with the dried fruit mixture.
9. Combine this with the batter and blend well.
10. Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake until a tester inserted near the center comes out with just a few crumbs attached, about 1 hour and 30 minutes. If browning too quickly, loosely cover cake with foil.
11. Let stand for 20 minutes. Turn pan over onto a rack. Let stand for 3 minutes; gently lift off the pan. Cool completely.
For Glazes:
1. Combine corn syrup and rum in a bowl. Place cake on a plate. Brush all of the rum glaze over the cake.
2. Double-wrap in foil and store at room temperature for at least 2 days (can be made ahead and stored in the refrigerator up to 3 months. Bring to room temperature before continuing).
3. Stir chocolate and butter in a heavy small saucepan over low heat until melted and smooth. Cool for 15 minutes.
4. Drizzle chocolate glaze over top of cake in decorative pattern. Garnish with orange peel strips and serve.
Quote: This is one fruitcake everyone will eat - guaranteed. It keeps up to three months, so make one now for the holidays.
From Bon Appetit magazine 9/92
Formatted for MM by Pegg Seevers 9/12/94
